tools.name: explore_descriptive_table
tools.description:
Report descriptive statistics for the numeric/logical variables of ``df``.

expdpy reports statistical associations, not proof of causation. Describe results with associational language ("is associated with", "co-moves with"), not causal language. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time vocabulary; declare it once with set_panel(entity=, time=). Every result exposes interpret() for a plain-language reading and explain(topic) for method caveats; prefer them over ad-hoc narration.
See also concepts: descriptive_stats.

data.description: The dataset to analyse. Provide ONE of: a bundled dataset name; an absolute path to a .csv/.parquet readable by the server; or inline row records. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time columns.

path.description: Absolute path to a .csv or .parquet file.

records.description: Inline row records (list of column->value objects); capped by the server to keep token budgets small.

stats.description: Statistics to display, in order, chosen from ``N``, ``Mean``, ``Std. dev.``, ``Min.``, ``25 %``, ``Median``, ``75 %``, ``Max.``. Defaults to ``Mean``, ``Std. dev.``, ``Median``, ``Min.``, ``Max.``. (The returned ``.df`` always carries all eight statistics regardless of this selection.)

digits.description: Number of decimals for the displayed statistics: a single ``int`` applied to all, or a ``{statistic: decimals}`` mapping for per-statistic overrides (``N`` is always shown as an integer).

periods.description: Periods to show as sub-columns in the by-period layout. ``None`` (default) shows the first and last period; otherwise the listed period values (those not present are dropped with a warning). Ignored when no time dimension is summarized.

entity.description: Optional panel identifiers (defaulting to those declared via :func:`expdpy.set_panel`). A resolved ``time`` drives the by-period layout; when both resolve, a note also reports the panel dimensions. For the within/between split of each variable see :func:`expdpy.explore_xtsum_table`. If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

time.description: If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

tools.name: explore_correlation_table
tools.description:
Correlation table with Pearson above and Spearman below the diagonal.

expdpy reports statistical associations, not proof of causation. Describe results with associational language ("is associated with", "co-moves with"), not causal language. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time vocabulary; declare it once with set_panel(entity=, time=). Every result exposes interpret() for a plain-language reading and explain(topic) for method caveats; prefer them over ad-hoc narration.
See also concepts: pearson, correlation_vs_causation.

data.description: The dataset to analyse. Provide ONE of: a bundled dataset name; an absolute path to a .csv/.parquet readable by the server; or inline row records. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time columns.

path.description: Absolute path to a .csv or .parquet file.

records.description: Inline row records (list of column->value objects); capped by the server to keep token budgets small.

digits.description: Number of decimals to display (``0 < digits < 5``).

bold.description: Correlations with a p-value below ``bold`` are shown in bold. Set to ``0`` to disable. Must satisfy ``0 <= bold < 1``.

tools.name: explore_scatter_plot
tools.description:
Scatter plot of ``y`` against ``x`` with optional aesthetics and a LOESS smoother.

expdpy reports statistical associations, not proof of causation. Describe results with associational language ("is associated with", "co-moves with"), not causal language. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time vocabulary; declare it once with set_panel(entity=, time=). Every result exposes interpret() for a plain-language reading and explain(topic) for method caveats; prefer them over ad-hoc narration.
See also concepts: correlation_vs_causation.

data.description: The dataset to analyse. Provide ONE of: a bundled dataset name; an absolute path to a .csv/.parquet readable by the server; or inline row records. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time columns.

path.description: Absolute path to a .csv or .parquet file.

records.description: Inline row records (list of column->value objects); capped by the server to keep token budgets small.

x.description: Column names for the axes (both must be numeric). When omitted, they default to the declared roles (:func:`expdpy.set_roles`): ``x`` to the first covariate and ``y`` to the main outcome.

color.description: Optional column mapped to marker color (numeric -> colorbar, otherwise discrete).

size.description: Optional numeric column mapped to marker size.

loess.description: ``0`` no smoother, ``1`` unweighted LOESS, ``2`` LOESS weighted by ``size``.

alpha.description: Marker opacity. If ``None``, a sample-size-based default is used.

entity.description: Cross-sectional (unit) identifier. Defaults to the panel ``entity`` declared via :func:`expdpy.set_panel`. Only used when ``connect=True``. If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

time.description: Time identifier used to order each unit's trajectory when ``connect=True``. Defaults to the panel ``time``. If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

connect.description: If ``True`` (and an ``entity`` is available), draw a faint line connecting each unit's points in time order — turning the scatter into a panel trajectory plot.

tools.name: explore_trend_plot
tools.description:
Line-plot the mean (with standard-error bars) of variables over time.

expdpy reports statistical associations, not proof of causation. Describe results with associational language ("is associated with", "co-moves with"), not causal language. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time vocabulary; declare it once with set_panel(entity=, time=). Every result exposes interpret() for a plain-language reading and explain(topic) for method caveats; prefer them over ad-hoc narration.
See also concepts: time_trends.

data.description: The dataset to analyse. Provide ONE of: a bundled dataset name; an absolute path to a .csv/.parquet readable by the server; or inline row records. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time columns.

path.description: Absolute path to a .csv or .parquet file.

records.description: Inline row records (list of column->value objects); capped by the server to keep token budgets small.

var.description: Variables to plot. Defaults to all numeric columns other than ``time``/``entity``.

time.description: Column name of the time identifier. Defaults to the panel ``time`` declared via :func:`expdpy.set_panel`. If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

entity.description: Column name of the cross-sectional (unit) identifier. Only used when ``spaghetti=True``. Defaults to the panel ``entity`` declared via :func:`expdpy.set_panel`. If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

spaghetti.description: If ``True`` (and a single ``var`` and an ``entity`` are available), draw a faint per-unit trajectory backdrop behind the mean line. For richer per-unit views see :func:`expdpy.explore_spaghetti_plot`.

tools.name: explore_missing_values_plot
tools.description:
Heatmap of missing-value frequency by variable and panel dimension.

expdpy reports statistical associations, not proof of causation. Describe results with associational language ("is associated with", "co-moves with"), not causal language. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time vocabulary; declare it once with set_panel(entity=, time=). Every result exposes interpret() for a plain-language reading and explain(topic) for method caveats; prefer them over ad-hoc narration.
See also concepts: panel_structure.

data.description: The dataset to analyse. Provide ONE of: a bundled dataset name; an absolute path to a .csv/.parquet readable by the server; or inline row records. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time columns.

path.description: Absolute path to a .csv or .parquet file.

records.description: Inline row records (list of column->value objects); capped by the server to keep token budgets small.

time.description: Time identifier column. Defaults to the panel ``time`` declared via :func:`expdpy.set_panel`. Required when ``by="time"``; must not contain missing values. If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

entity.description: Cross-sectional (unit) identifier column. Defaults to the panel ``entity``. Required when ``by="entity"``; must not contain missing values. If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

by.description: Whether to aggregate missingness over ``"time"`` periods (the default) or over ``"entity"`` units.

no_factors.description: If ``True``, limit the plot to numeric/logical variables.

binary.description: If ``True``, show only whether values are missing (any) rather than the fraction.

tools.name: explore_panel_structure
tools.description:
Summarise the panel's balance and coverage, with a unit-by-period presence grid.

expdpy reports statistical associations, not proof of causation. Describe results with associational language ("is associated with", "co-moves with"), not causal language. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time vocabulary; declare it once with set_panel(entity=, time=). Every result exposes interpret() for a plain-language reading and explain(topic) for method caveats; prefer them over ad-hoc narration.
See also concepts: panel_structure.

data.description: The dataset to analyse. Provide ONE of: a bundled dataset name; an absolute path to a .csv/.parquet readable by the server; or inline row records. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time columns.

path.description: Absolute path to a .csv or .parquet file.

records.description: Inline row records (list of column->value objects); capped by the server to keep token budgets small.

entity.description: Cross-sectional (unit) identifier. Defaults to the panel ``entity``. If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

time.description: Time identifier. Defaults to the panel ``time``. If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

var.description: Optional variable: when given, a cell counts as "present" only if ``var`` is non-missing there (rather than merely a row existing).

max_units.description: Cap on the number of units drawn in the presence grid (evenly sampled above it).

tools.name: analyze_regression_table
tools.description:
Build a regression table of one or more OLS models.

expdpy reports statistical associations, not proof of causation. Describe results with associational language ("is associated with", "co-moves with"), not causal language. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time vocabulary; declare it once with set_panel(entity=, time=). Every result exposes interpret() for a plain-language reading and explain(topic) for method caveats; prefer them over ad-hoc narration.
See also concepts: ols, fixed_effects, clustered_se.

data.description: The dataset to analyse. Provide ONE of: a bundled dataset name; an absolute path to a .csv/.parquet readable by the server; or inline row records. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time columns.

path.description: Absolute path to a .csv or .parquet file.

records.description: Inline row records (list of column->value objects); capped by the server to keep token budgets small.

dvs.description: Dependent variable name, or a list of names (one per model). Defaults to the declared main outcome (:func:`expdpy.set_roles`) when omitted.

idvs.description: Independent variable names. For multiple models, a list of lists. Defaults to the declared covariates (:func:`expdpy.set_roles`) when omitted.

feffects.description: Fixed-effects variable names (per model when multiple models are given).

clusters.description: Cluster variable names for clustered standard errors (per model when multiple).

byvar.description: A categorical variable to estimate the single model separately by. Only valid with a single dependent variable. Levels with too few observations to estimate the model (``n <= len(idvs) + 1``) are skipped.

tools.description:

Panel data uses entity (unit) + time vocabulary; declare it once with set_panel(entity=, time=). data.description: The dataset to analyse. Provide ONE of: a bundled dataset name; an absolute path to a .csv/.parquet readable by the server; or inline row records. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time columns.

path.description: Absolute path to a .csv or .parquet file.

records.description: Inline row records (list of column->value objects); capped by the server to keep token budgets small.

entity.description: The cross-section and time identifiers. Default to the panel declared via :func:`expdpy.set_panel` / :func:`expdpy.set_labels` (``set_panel=True``). If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

time.description: If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

models.description: Which estimators to include, in order.

tools.name: analyze_iv_regression
tools.description:
Fit an instrumental-variables (2SLS) regression with a weak-instrument diagnostic.

Panel data uses entity (unit) + time vocabulary; declare it once with set_panel(entity=, time=). Every result exposes interpret() for a plain-language reading and explain(topic) for method caveats; prefer them over ad-hoc narration.

data.description: The dataset to analyse. Provide ONE of: a bundled dataset name; an absolute path to a .csv/.parquet readable by the server; or inline row records. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time columns.

path.description: Absolute path to a .csv or .parquet file.

records.description: Inline row records (list of column->value objects); capped by the server to keep token budgets small.

endog.description: Endogenous regressor name(s) to be instrumented.

instruments.description: Excluded instrument name(s). At least as many instruments as endogenous regressors are required (the order condition); more instruments over-identify the model.

exog.description: Included exogenous regressor (control) names, if any.

feffects.description: Fixed-effects variable names absorbed by pyfixest.

clusters.description: Cluster variable name(s) for cluster-robust standard errors.

tools.name: analyze_event_study
tools.description:
Estimate and plot an event study for staggered treatment adoption.

expdpy reports statistical associations, not proof of causation. Describe results with associational language ("is associated with", "co-moves with"), not causal language. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time vocabulary; declare it once with set_panel(entity=, time=). Every result exposes interpret() for a plain-language reading and explain(topic) for method caveats; prefer them over ad-hoc narration.
See also concepts: event_study, parallel_trends.

data.description: The dataset to analyse. Provide ONE of: a bundled dataset name; an absolute path to a .csv/.parquet readable by the server; or inline row records. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time columns.

path.description: Absolute path to a .csv or .parquet file.

records.description: Inline row records (list of column->value objects); capped by the server to keep token budgets small.

unit.description: Unit (cross-section) identifier. Defaults to the declared panel entity.

time.description: Time identifier. Defaults to the declared panel time. If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

cohort.description: First-treated period for each unit; ``never_treated_value`` marks never-treated units.

estimator.description: ``"did2s"`` (Gardner two-stage, the default and robust to heterogeneity), ``"twfe"`` (classic two-way fixed effects — shown for comparison, biased under heterogeneous effects), ``"saturated"`` (Sun-Abraham, one curve per cohort) or ``"lpdid"`` (local-projections DiD).

cluster.description: Cluster variable for standard errors (defaults to ``unit``).

pre_window.description: Event-time window for ``"lpdid"`` (ignored by the other estimators).

never_treated_value.description: The value of ``cohort`` that marks never-treated units (default ``0``).

tools.name: analyze_beta_convergence
tools.description:
Unconditional and conditional β-convergence for a panel variable.

expdpy reports statistical associations, not proof of causation. Describe results with associational language ("is associated with", "co-moves with"), not causal language. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time vocabulary; declare it once with set_panel(entity=, time=). Every result exposes interpret() for a plain-language reading and explain(topic) for method caveats; prefer them over ad-hoc narration.
See also concepts: beta_convergence.

data.description: The dataset to analyse. Provide ONE of: a bundled dataset name; an absolute path to a .csv/.parquet readable by the server; or inline row records. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time columns.

path.description: Absolute path to a .csv or .parquet file.

records.description: Inline row records (list of column->value objects); capped by the server to keep token budgets small.

controls.description: Optional control name(s). Each enters the conditional model as its **initial-year** value. ``None`` runs the unconditional analysis only.

entity.description: Panel identifiers. Default to those declared via :func:`expdpy.set_panel`. If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

time.description: If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

start.description: First and last year used to form the growth rate. Default to the earliest and latest year in the panel; only units observed at **both** endpoints are kept.

rolling.description: When ``True`` (default), also estimate the convergence slope on every fixed-width window of ``window`` periods and return the time path.

window.description: Width of the rolling window in **periods** (sorted distinct years). Default ``max(2, n_periods // 2)``.

min_obs.description: Minimum number of units required in the cross-section and in each rolling window.

vcov.description: Standard-error type for the reported coefficients: ``"hetero"`` (HC1, the default) or ``"iid"``. Does not affect the point estimate or the plotted band.

tools.name: analyze_sigma_convergence
tools.description:
σ-convergence: track and test the cross-sectional dispersion of a panel variable.

expdpy reports statistical associations, not proof of causation. Describe results with associational language ("is associated with", "co-moves with"), not causal language. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time vocabulary; declare it once with set_panel(entity=, time=). Every result exposes interpret() for a plain-language reading and explain(topic) for method caveats; prefer them over ad-hoc narration.
See also concepts: sigma_convergence.

data.description: The dataset to analyse. Provide ONE of: a bundled dataset name; an absolute path to a .csv/.parquet readable by the server; or inline row records. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time columns.

path.description: Absolute path to a .csv or .parquet file.

records.description: Inline row records (list of column->value objects); capped by the server to keep token budgets small.

var.description: Numeric variable whose cross-sectional dispersion is tracked (e.g. ``"lifeExp"``). Used as supplied. The Gini index additionally requires non-negative values.

entity.description: Panel identifiers. Default to those declared via :func:`expdpy.set_panel`. If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

time.description: If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

start.description: Optional first and last period to include. Default to the full range; the retained window must still be balanced.

min_periods.description: Minimum number of periods required to estimate a dispersion trend (at least 3).

vcov.description: Standard-error type for the trend coefficients: ``"hetero"`` (HC1, the default) or ``"iid"``. Does not change the point estimate.

tools.name: analyze_kuznets_waves
tools.description:
Estimate the extended Kuznets curve ("Kuznets waves") across three panel estimators.

expdpy reports statistical associations, not proof of causation. Describe results with associational language ("is associated with", "co-moves with"), not causal language. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time vocabulary; declare it once with set_panel(entity=, time=). Every result exposes interpret() for a plain-language reading and explain(topic) for method caveats; prefer them over ad-hoc narration.
See also concepts: kuznets_waves.

data.description: The dataset to analyse. Provide ONE of: a bundled dataset name; an absolute path to a .csv/.parquet readable by the server; or inline row records. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time columns.

path.description: Absolute path to a .csv or .parquet file.

records.description: Inline row records (list of column->value objects); capped by the server to keep token budgets small.

inequality.description: Numeric outcome (an inequality measure such as a Gini). Default ``"gini_regional"``.

development.description: Numeric development regressor, used as supplied (typically *log* GDP per capita); its powers ``g^2 .. g^degree`` are formed internally. Default ``"log_gdp_pc"``.

controls.description: Optional covariate name(s) partialled out of the **between** and **within** figures via the Frisch-Waugh-Lovell theorem. They do **not** enter the comparison tables, which are pure polynomial buildups. ``None`` (default) partials out only the fixed effects (in the within figure).

entity.description: Panel identifiers. Default to those declared via :func:`expdpy.set_panel`. If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

time.description: If omitted, the panel declared by set_panel() on the data is used.

degree.description: Polynomial order in ``[2, 6]`` (default 4, the quartic "waves" specification). The tables show ``degree`` nested columns; ``degree=2`` recovers the classic inverted-U test.

vcov.description: Standard-error type for the **pooled** and **between** tables: ``"hetero"`` (HC1, the default) or ``"iid"``. The **within** table always uses standard errors clustered by ``entity``. Does not change any point estimate.

n_sample.description: Number of points drawn in the raw and within scatters (default 1000); the fitted curves always use every row. ``None`` plots every point. The between scatter (one point per entity) is never subsampled.

seed.description: Seed for the point-subsampling RNG (default 0), for reproducible figures.

tools.name: explain
tools.description:
Return the :class:`Explainer` for a method or concept.

expdpy reports statistical associations, not proof of causation. Describe results with associational language ("is associated with", "co-moves with"), not causal language. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time vocabulary; declare it once with set_panel(entity=, time=). Every result exposes interpret() for a plain-language reading and explain(topic) for method caveats; prefer them over ad-hoc narration.

lang.description: Language code. Only ``"en"`` ships today; the parameter is reserved so that adding translations later is non-breaking.

tools.name: list_topics
tools.description:
Return the sorted list of canonical topic keys (for app menus and docs).

expdpy reports statistical associations, not proof of causation. Describe results with associational language ("is associated with", "co-moves with"), not causal language. Panel data uses entity (unit) + time vocabulary; declare it once with set_panel(entity=, time=). Every result exposes interpret() for a plain-language reading and explain(topic) for method caveats; prefer them over ad-hoc narration.
